skill.name: google-agents-cli-workflow
skill.description: Guides Codex, Claude, and Claude Code through the Google Agents CLI workflow for ADK agent projects, from requirements and scaffolding through evaluation, deployment, publishing, and observability.
skill.summary: Run a structured Google ADK agent lifecycle with agents-cli.
skill.author: google

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced a critical heuristic because the Markdown contains many inline command examples, URLs, filesystem paths, and credential-related guidance. Review found no malicious code, no prompt injection attempt, and no evidence of Ruby backtick execution; the real risk is that the skill intentionally guides assistants to run agents-cli, uv, git, Terraform, gcloud, kubectl, and deployment workflows. Publish with warnings about command execution, network access, filesystem modification, and secret handling.

medium_findings.title: Workflow Instructions Execute Local and Cloud Commands
medium_findings.description: The static command-execution alerts are false positives for Ruby backtick execution because the matches are Markdown command references. The skill still intentionally instructs assistants to run project scaffolding, install, test, deploy, Terraform, gcloud, and kubectl workflows. These commands can modify local projects or cloud resources, so users need explicit review before execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines directly list command workflows and deployment commands. The risk is operational command execution, not malicious Ruby execution.

medium_findings.title: Network and Repository Access During Agent Setup
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs installation from package tooling, cloning Google sample repositories, fetching ADK documentation, and using web search for troubleshooting. These network actions are legitimate for the workflow, but they can disclose environment metadata and pull remote content into the development process.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network destinations and commands are explicit in the Markdown. They appear tied to normal setup and troubleshooting rather than exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Credential-Adjacent Environment Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill tells assistants to preserve .env files, mentions api_key configuration, and discusses API key authentication. It also recommends GCP Secret Manager for secrets. This is not credential theft, but it requires care to avoid exposing or rewriting sensitive configuration.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines clearly mention .env files, API keys, and authentication behavior. The guidance is protective overall, so the issue is handling risk rather than confirmed misuse.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts do not correspond to cryptographic code in the reviewed lines. The cited content is skill metadata, model guidance, evaluation command names, cloud storage destinations, or command reference text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found documentation text and CLI command names, not MD5, SHA-1, or other weak cryptographic implementation. No evidence found of cryptographic operations.

low_findings.title: Heuristic Dangerous Combination Is Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The scanner combined command examples, URLs, filesystem paths, and credential-related words into a critical heuristic. Manual review found no exfiltration workflow, obfuscated payload, hidden instruction, or malicious intent in the skill files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious categories are present, but the semantic context is developer workflow documentation. No evidence found of credential exfiltration or covert command execution.

content.user_title: Build Google ADK Agents with Agents CLI
content.value_statement: Agent projects need a repeatable path from idea to tested deployment. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured Google Agents CLI workflow for ADK development.
actual_capabilities: Guides requirements discovery before scaffolding an ADK agent project.
Maps agent lifecycle phases from scaffold through build, evaluation, deployment, publishing, and observability.
Directs use of Google Agents CLI commands for setup, local runs, linting, evaluation, and deployment.
Provides sample-selection guidance for common ADK project patterns and reference implementations.
Documents troubleshooting steps for models, CLI failures, environment variables, and deployment workflows.
Encourages human approval before production deployment and careful preservation of existing project code.
limitations: Requires google-agents-cli and related tools to be installed in the developer environment.
Does not replace review of generated code, Terraform plans, cloud permissions, or deployment changes.
May need network access to install tools, clone samples, fetch documentation, or query cloud services.
Focuses on Google ADK and Google Cloud workflows, not every agent framework.

use_cases.title: Start a New ADK Agent
use_cases.description: Capture requirements, choose an appropriate scaffold path, and create an agent project with evaluation structure included.

use_cases.title: Improve an Existing Agent Project
use_cases.description: Enhance an existing codebase with agents-cli conventions while preserving unrelated source files and configuration.

use_cases.title: Prepare an Agent for Release
use_cases.description: Run evaluations, review deployment options, publish when needed, and plan observability after release.

prompt_templates.title: Plan My Agent Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Google Agents CLI workflow to help me define an ADK agent. Ask the required discovery questions before proposing scaffolding.

prompt_templates.title: Scaffold an Agent Safely
prompt_templates.prompt: Use agents-cli workflow guidance to scaffold a new ADK agent project. Explain each command before running it and preserve my stated requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate and Fix Behavior
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide me through evaluating this ADK agent with agents-cli. Start with a small dataset, inspect failures, and suggest targeted fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Production Deployment
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this ADK agent for deployment readiness. Check evaluation status, environment variables, deployment target, CI/CD needs, publishing, and observability.

output_examples.input: I want to build an agent that answers internal policy questions.
output_examples.output: The assistant first asks about data sources, safety constraints, deployment preference, and evaluation criteria. It then recommends a scaffold path and a small initial evaluation set.

output_examples.input: My deployed agent fails after I changed its prompt.
output_examples.output: The assistant separates code tests from behavior evaluation, reproduces the issue with agents-cli run, adds an eval case, and suggests a focused prompt or tool fix.

output_examples.input: We are ready to publish the agent for Gemini Enterprise users.
output_examples.output: The assistant checks approval, deployment target, authentication, registration requirements, and monitoring steps before any publish command is run.

best_practices: Confirm requirements and safety constraints before scaffolding or writing agent code.
Run behavior evaluations with agents-cli before deployment or publishing.
Review all commands, cloud changes, and secret handling before execution.
anti_patterns: Skipping scaffolding and manually recreating agents-cli project structure.
Changing model names, configuration, or .env values without an explicit request.
Using pytest assertions for nondeterministic LLM response content instead of evals.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build, evaluate, deploy, publish, and observe Google ADK agents with Google Agents CLI.

faq.question: Can it work with Codex and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is written for coding agents including Codex, Claude, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it run commands automatically?
faq.answer: It provides command guidance. Users should review commands before execution, especially deployment and infrastructure commands.

faq.question: Is this limited to Google Cloud?
faq.answer: The workflow focuses on Google ADK, Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform, and Google Cloud deployment targets.

faq.question: How should secrets be handled?
faq.answer: The skill recommends preserving existing environment configuration and using GCP Secret Manager for secrets where possible.

faq.question: Why is evaluation emphasized?
faq.answer: Agent behavior is nondeterministic. The workflow uses agents-cli evaluation to test behavior instead of brittle response-content unit tests.
